note_html:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old female, presents with a chronic history of asthma, experiencing frequent exacerbations, particularly when she gets sick with a cold, and occasionally when exposed to her aunt's cat. She reports using her albuterol inhaler daily, taking two puffs every 4-6 hours as needed, and also occasionally using steroids after urgent care visits. She has had several urgent care visits and one emergency department visit in the past year, with the most recent exacerbation occurring about a month ago. She denies smoking, vaping, or marijuana use and has no history of seasonal allergies. She enjoys hiking and outdoor activities but has to be careful due to her asthma symptoms.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of asthma, with a past hospitalization at age 8 due to asthma symptoms. She has had multiple urgent care visits and one emergency department visit in the past year.

Review of systems: The patient reports coughing and wheezing symptoms, but no other symptoms in other organs.

Current medications: 
Ventolin (albuterol) 2 puffs every 4-6 hours p.r.n. for asthma
Flovent (fluticasone) 110 micrograms 1 puff twice per day for asthma

Objective:
Vital signs: 
Blood pressure: 128/82
Pulse rate: 16
Oxygen saturation: 99% on room air
Respiratory rate: 16

Physical exam: 
Diminished lung sounds throughout with occasional slight expiratory wheeze bilaterally
Trachea is midline
No lymphadenopathy
No carotid bruit
Strong bilateral pulses
No clubbing on fingertips
Brisk capillary refill

Test Results: 
Pulmonary function test results consistent with asthma

Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has moderate intermittent asthma, which is confirmed by pulmonary function test results and clinical presentation.

Problem list: Asthma (active); History of asthma hospitalization (past); History of asthma exacerbations (active)

Plan:
I prescribed Flovent (fluticasone) 110 micrograms 1 puff twice per day to help prevent daily symptoms and reduce the need for rescue inhaler use. I advised the patient to continue using Ventolin (albuterol) 2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed for acute exacerbations. I will put together an asthma action plan to help manage symptoms and identify triggers. I will see the patient in 30 days to reassess symptoms and adjust treatment as needed.

Follow-up: The patient will be seen in 30 days to reassess symptoms and adjust treatment as needed.
